seo.title: AI Agent Testing Strategies in OpenAI Agents SDK Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent testing strategies in OpenAI Agents SDK agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: AI Agent Testing Strategies failures in OpenAI Agents SDK manifest as unreliable multi-agent handoffs, unvalidated tool outputs, and guardrail bypasses that cascade through agent chains. These issues appear specifically in the SDK's `Agent.handoff()` patterns and `Agent.run()` execution flows, where tracing gaps hide looping behaviors and prompt injections evade `guardrails.validate()` checks. Without systematic testing, production agents degrade into infinite loops or produce hallucinated responses from untrusted tool data.

howItManifests.scenario: Agent handoff loops between specialized agents
howItManifests.symptom: Agents ping-pong indefinitely between `handoff()` calls without progress
howItManifests.impact: Infinite execution loops consuming API quotas and timing out

howItManifests.symptom: `guardrails.validate()` returns True despite malicious 'ignore previous instructions' payloads
howItManifests.impact: Agents execute unauthorized actions or leak sensitive data

howItManifests.scenario: Tool output not validated before agent consumption
howItManifests.symptom: Raw `tool.call()` JSON passed directly to `agent.run()` without schema validation
howItManifests.impact: Agents hallucinate from malformed or malicious tool responses

howItManifests.scenario: Tracing gaps in multi-agent handoff chains
howItManifests.impact: Impossible to debug cascading failures in production handoff chains

detection.name: Handoff Loop Detector
detection.description: Scans agent execution traces for repeated handoff patterns exceeding threshold

detection.name: Prompt Injection Scanner
detection.description: Detects jailbreak patterns in inputs that bypass guardrails.validate()

detection.name: Tool Output Validator
detection.description: Checks if tool responses match expected JSON schemas before agent consumption

fixes.name: Circuit Breaker Handoff Guard
fixes.description: Prevents infinite loops by tracking handoff state and enforcing max iterations

fixes.name: Hardened Input Sanitizer
fixes.description: Strips jailbreak attempts before guardrail validation

fixes.name: Tool Response Validator
fixes.description: Enforces JSON schema validation before passing to agent

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Testing Strategies Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates directly as output guardrails on OpenAI Agents SDK agents, automatically detecting handoff loops, prompt injections, and validation failures across multi-agent chains. Using `VexGuardrail(agent)` wrappers, Vex captures 100% of agent executions with full tracing context, applying ML-powered anomaly detection trained specifically on OpenAI Agents SDK patterns. 

Remediation happens inline: Vex can block malicious handoffs, sanitize injected prompts, and validate tool outputs before they reach downstream agents. Production dashboards show failure cascades with one-click fixes that deploy across your entire agent fleet. The SDK integration requires zero code changes beyond `pip install openai-agents vex-sdk` and adding VexGuardrail to agent constructors.
vexAutomation.code:
```python
from openai_agents import Agent, Runner
from vex_sdk import VexGuardrail

# Install: pip install openai-agents vex-sdk

# Wrap existing agents with Vex
deforensics_team = Agent(name="Forensics", model="gpt-4o")
researcher = Agent(name="Researcher", model="gpt-4o")

# Zero-config Vex integration
forensics_team = VexGuardrail(forensics_team)
researcher = VexGuardrail(researcher)

runner = Runner(agents=[forensics_team, researcher])
result = runner.run('Analyze this contract for risks')

# Vex auto-detects loops, injections, validation failures
print(result.vex_report)
```

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Add `pip install openai-agents vex-sdk` and wrap your agents to eliminate testing failures instantly.
